subject: A fresh subagent's orientation statement produced after reading ONLY skills/myfi/SKILL.md — does it know how to reach the toolkit (CLI + MCP + per-project DB), which flock agent to dispatch and when, the four entry-point commands, and the LLM-routing law?

dimensions.desc: Correctly names bin/myfi-toolkit CLI verbs, the myfi-mcp MCP tool prefix (mcp__plugin_myfi_myfi-toolkit__*), and the per-project .myfi/myfi.db — concrete, not a vague 'there is a toolkit'.
Names all six agents (advisor, auditor, quant, designer, worker, trader) and states when to reach for each, including that trader is scaffold-only with no live execution.
States that every model call routes through services/llm to local Claude Code and never a hosted inference API (not Anthropic's, not OpenAI's, not any provider's).
A subagent with zero other context could act on this immediately — dispatch the right agent, call the right CLI/MCP verb, invoke the right command — without re-reading the skill.

guidance: Reward concrete, checkable orientation (tool names, verbs, agent handles) over generic paraphrase. Penalize a summary that describes the skill's existence without letting the reader act, drops or lumps together any of the six agents, mischaracterizes trader as capable of live execution, or omits/misstates the LLM-routing law.
